synthesis.summary: COIN is up 11.3% today with no supplied news directly explaining the move. The two news items cover broad market sentiment and an unrelated ticker (CRCL). The likely drivers are crypto market price action (Bitcoin/Ethereum moves) or broader risk-on flows benefiting crypto-exposed equities, but none of that is confirmed by the provided news. Relative volume is below average (0.83x), suggesting this is more price-driven than a high-conviction institutional event.

Datadog acquired Adaptive ML, a reinforcement learning startup, which is driving today's 3% gain and likely contributing to the broader 20% 5-day run as AI/ML capability expansion re-rates the growth thesis. However, the low relative volume (0.39) suggests the move may be more momentum-driven than a broad market conviction buy on this specific deal.

Nike reported quarterly results that beat enough to trigger a +3% pop on 2.8x normal volume, but Wall Street commentary signals the underlying turnaround is still in early stages — limiting how much of the earnings beat re-rates the forward model. The move is a relief rally off a depressed base (RSI ~45) rather than a full thesis reversal.

Reddit surged 12% as a Benzinga exclusive highlighted the company's growing role as a training data supplier for AI models, spotlighting its existing deals with Google and OpenAI and framing them as the floor rather than the ceiling of a broader licensing revenue stream. The article reframes Reddit's data moat as a structural monetization asset, likely pulling in momentum buyers and re-rating the forward model. Volume was below average (rel vol 0.89), suggesting this is more sentiment-driven repricing than institutional accumulation.
